tasks.subject: [relay-panel-redesign] [P1] Add binding permission fields to shared schema
tasks.description:
## Overview

Add `canInitiate`, `canReply`, and `canReceive` permission fields to `AdapterBindingSchema` in the shared package. This is a prerequisite for Phase 7 server enforcement and Phase 7 client UI, but can be done independently since all fields have backward-compatible defaults.

## Files to Modify

- `packages/shared/src/relay-adapter-schemas.ts`

## Implementation

In `packages/shared/src/relay-adapter-schemas.ts`, add three fields to `AdapterBindingSchema` (after `label` and before `createdAt`):

```typescript
export const AdapterBindingSchema = z
  .object({
    id: z.string().uuid(),
    adapterId: z.string(),
    agentId: z.string(),
    chatId: z.string().optional(),
    channelType: ChannelTypeSchema.optional(),
    sessionStrategy: SessionStrategySchema.default('per-chat'),
    label: z.string().default(''),
    canInitiate: z.boolean().default(false),
    canReply: z.boolean().default(true),
    canReceive: z.boolean().default(true),
    createdAt: z.string().datetime(),
    updatedAt: z.string().datetime(),
  })
  .openapi('AdapterBinding');
```

Field semantics:

| Field | Default | Meaning |
|-------|---------|--------|
| `canInitiate` | `false` | Agent can send unprompted messages to external platform. Conservative default prevents overnight spam. |
| `canReply` | `true` | Agent can respond when a human sends a message through the adapter. |
| `canReceive` | `true` | Inbound messages from the adapter are delivered to the agent. |

All fields have defaults, making this a backward-compatible schema addition. Existing bindings stored on disk will parse correctly with Zod defaults applied.

The `CreateBindingRequestSchema` already uses `.omit()` on `id`, `createdAt`, `updatedAt` — the new fields with defaults will be included automatically via `CreateBindingRequestSchema`.

## Verification

Run `pnpm typecheck` to verify that:
- The `AdapterBinding` type now includes the three new fields
- `CreateBindingRequest` includes them with defaults
- No existing code breaks (all fields have defaults)
- Existing test fixtures in `apps/server/src/services/relay/__tests__/binding-store.test.ts` still compile

## Acceptance Criteria

- `AdapterBindingSchema` includes `canInitiate: z.boolean().default(false)`
- `AdapterBindingSchema` includes `canReply: z.boolean().default(true)`
- `AdapterBindingSchema` includes `canReceive: z.boolean().default(true)`
- `CreateBindingRequestSchema` includes the new fields (inherits from `AdapterBindingSchema.omit()`)
- Existing bindings without these fields parse correctly via Zod defaults
- `pnpm typecheck` passes across all packages
tasks.activeForm: Adding binding permission fields to shared schema

tasks.subject: [relay-panel-redesign] [P7] Add permissions UI to BindingDialog and AdapterCard
tasks.description:
## Overview Add a collapsible "Advanced" section to the BindingDialog with permission toggles and move session strategy into it. Show non-default permission indicators on binding rows in AdapterCard. ## Files to Modify - `apps/client/src/layers/features/mesh/ui/BindingDialog.tsx` — add Advanced section with permissions - `apps/client/src/layers/features/relay/ui/AdapterCard.tsx` — permission indicators on binding rows - `apps/client/src/layers/features/relay/ui/AdapterBindingRow.tsx` — update if binding rows are rendered here ## Implementation: BindingDialog Advanced Section In `BindingDialog.tsx`, add a collapsible "Advanced" section below the existing binding fields. Use shadcn `Collapsible` primitive: ```tsx import { Collapsible, CollapsibleContent, CollapsibleTrigger } from '@/layers/shared/ui'; import { ChevronRight, Shield } from 'lucide-react'; // Inside the dialog form, after the main fields: Advanced {/* Permission toggles */}
{/* Session strategy selector (moved from always-visible) */} {/* ... existing session strategy UI ... */} ``` Update `BindingFormValues` type to include the new permission fields: ```typescript export interface BindingFormValues { adapterId: string; agentId: string; sessionStrategy: string; label: string; chatId?: string; channelType?: 'dm' | 'group' | 'channel' | 'thread'; canInitiate?: boolean; canReply?: boolean; canReceive?: boolean; } ``` The Advanced section is collapsed by default. ## Implementation: AdapterCard Permission Indicators In `AdapterCard.tsx` (or `AdapterBindingRow.tsx` if binding rows are rendered there), show permission indicators only when they deviate from defaults: - `canInitiate: true` shows a small shield icon with tooltip "Can initiate messages" - `canReply: false` shows a muted badge "Reply disabled" - `canReceive: false` shows a muted badge "Receive disabled" When all permissions are at defaults, show nothing. ```tsx {binding.canInitiate && ( Can initiate messages )} {binding.canReply === false && ( Reply disabled )} {binding.canReceive === false && ( Receive disabled )} ``` Also hide the session strategy badge ("Per Chat") when it matches the default (`per-chat`). Only show non-default strategies: ```tsx {binding.sessionStrategy !== 'per-chat' && ( {binding.sessionStrategy === 'per-user' ? 'Per User' : 'Stateless'} )} ``` ## Implementation: Update BindingStore.update() In `apps/server/src/services/relay/binding-store.ts`, update the `update` method's type to accept permission fields: ```typescript async update( id: string, updates: Partial>, ): Promise { ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - BindingDialog has a collapsible "Advanced" section - Advanced section contains three permission toggles with correct defaults - Session strategy is inside the Advanced section (not always visible) - Permission toggles control `canInitiate`, `canReply`, `canReceive` form values - AdapterCard binding rows show shield icon when `canInitiate=true` - AdapterCard binding rows show "Reply disabled" badge when `canReply=false` - AdapterCard binding rows show "Receive disabled" badge when `canReceive=false` - Session badge hidden when strategy is `per-chat` (default) - Default permission state shows no indicators (clean, quiet UI)
tasks.activeForm: Adding permissions UI to BindingDialog and AdapterCard
